items.headline: Frontier USA JV recapitalised: Hudson Bay Capital joins; rights offering priced at $5.481; DOE consent obtained
items.summary: An 8-K + 424B5 (Jun 30) set out a binding amended term sheet making Frontier Power USA a three-way JV — Eos, Cerberus ($100M + founder equity + 20.0M warrants) and new partner Hudson Bay Capital ($50M Class C + a registered direct purchase of Eos stock/warrants + 10.0M warrants). The rights offering is now priced: $5.481 per unit (1 share + 0.4388 warrant, $5.481 strike, 10-yr). DOE and Cerberus-lender consents obtained; remaining is completing the rights offering.

items.headline: Ofgem LDES Window 1 minded-to decisions — only 16 of 73 projects provisionally awarded; Frontier UK largely excluded
items.summary: Provisional cap & floor awards skew to pumped hydro + long-duration; of Frontier UK's ~16 projects (~2.6 GW, all 8-hour) only one — Frontier Legacy, 65 MW — made the provisional list. The 'VFB/Zn' category does not confirm Eos vs Invinity tech. Consultation to Aug 7; final awards Autumn 2026; a Window 2 is expected.

items.headline: Frontier Power USA Engages KKR Capital Markets to Support Scaled Deployment of Long-Duration Energy Storage
items.summary: FPUSA engaged KKR Capital Markets as structuring agent for construction finance, tax equity and long-term project finance across its 2 GWh pipeline — de-risking FPUSA's capitalisation alongside Cerberus' $100M equity anchor.

items.headline: Independent Fire Testing Confirms Eos Z3 Battery System Exhibited No Thermal Runaway, No Sustained Fire, and No Propagation Under Abuse Testing; Company Receives ISO 14001 Certification
items.summary: Energy Safety Response Group testing found no thermal runaway, no sustained fire, and no propagation under direct flame impingement and overcharge abuse, supporting safe siting of long-duration storage in dense urban environments…

items.headline: Eos Energy Enterprises Establishes Strategic Entry into Germany Through Exclusive Long-Duration Storage Partnership
items.summary: Binding Master Supply Agreement secures a 750 MWh capacity commitment with a pathway to scale up to 2 GWh of Indensity™ deployments across Germany, Austria and Switzerland through 2031 First projects under a previously executed purchase…

items.summary: Frontier Power USA is expected to be a purpose-built independent development and investment company utilizing Eos’ vertically integrated technology stack to accelerate gigawatt-scale deployment of American-made energy storage 2 GWh Firm…
